title: The Shadow in the Study
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Astrid Markham, renowned literary critic, was found dead in the victim's dimly lit home office. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Cassandra Wellesley
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Elijah Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Lysander Blackwood helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Cassandra Wellesley and Astrid Markham were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Lysander Blackwood and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Lysander Blackwood
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Elijah Pembroke move the chair clear of the doorway. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Elijah Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Astrid Markham has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Elijah Pembroke and Lysander Blackwood both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Cassandra Wellesley's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Book
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged between the cover and first page of the book on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Cassandra Wellesley has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Cassandra Wellesley killed Astrid Markham because a long-buried family secret that threatened to destroy Astrid's reputation and livelihood because.
solution.method: Astrid Markham was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Cassandra Wellesley claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Elijah Pembroke and Lysander Blackwood left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp and the black and gold fragment recovered from the book place Cassandra Wellesley inside afterwards. Cassandra Wellesley therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Astrid Markham and reach the lamp during the murder window.
